academic_programs.name: MSc in Applied Data Science
academic_programs.overview: A two-year program focused on practical data science skills for industry and research applications.
academic_programs.curriculumSummary: Core modules in statistics, machine learning, data engineering, and visualization; electives in AI, natural language processing, and responsible data science; capstone industry project.
academic_programs.entryRequirements: Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field, proficiency in programming (Python or R), and English language proficiency (IELTS 6.5 or equivalent).

academic_programs.name: MSc in Data Science and Big Data Analytics
academic_programs.overview: Specialized training in large-scale data processing, cloud computing, and advanced analytics.
academic_programs.curriculumSummary: Courses in distributed systems, large-scale machine learning, database systems, and data ethics; integrated internship in tech or finance.
academic_programs.entryRequirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics, or related field; experience with programming and linear algebra.

academic_programs.name: MSc in Data Science and Artificial Intelligence
academic_programs.overview: An interdisciplinary program at the intersection of data science and artificial intelligence.
academic_programs.curriculumSummary: Machine learning theory, deep learning, reinforcement learning, causal inference, and AI ethics; research thesis in collaboration with an institute lab.
academic_programs.entryRequirements: Strong mathematical background, prior coursework in algorithms and probability, and demonstrated interest in AI.

academic_programs.name: MSc in Data Science (German Track)
academic_programs.overview: German-language track focusing on data science methods and applications in Europe.
academic_programs.curriculumSummary: Core modules in statistical learning, databases, and data visualization with German-language seminars and projects.
academic_programs.entryRequirements: Bachelorabschluss in einem quantitativen Fach und Deutschkenntnisse auf Niveau C1.

academic_programs.name: MSc in Machine Learning
academic_programs.overview: Advanced training in core and modern machine learning techniques with strong theoretical foundations.
academic_programs.curriculumSummary: Statistical learning theory, deep learning, probabilistic modeling, optimization, and research seminars; independent research project.
academic_programs.entryRequirements: Bachelor’s degree in mathematics, computer science, or related; strong background in calculus, linear algebra, and programming.

academic_programs.name: MSc in Integrative Neuroscience
academic_programs.overview: A research-focused program covering systems, cognitive, and computational neuroscience.
academic_programs.curriculumSummary: Neuroanatomy, systems neuroscience, cognitive neuroscience, neuroimaging methods, and lab rotations in partner labs.
academic_programs.entryRequirements: Bachelor’s degree in neuroscience, biology, psychology, or related field; motivation letter and two references.

academic_programs.name: BSc in Computer Science
academic_programs.overview: Undergraduate program covering foundational topics in computer science.
academic_programs.curriculumSummary: Programming, data structures, algorithms, databases, operating systems, and introductory AI.
academic_programs.entryRequirements: Secondary school diploma with strong performance in mathematics.

academic_programs.name: PhD in Computer Science
academic_programs.overview: Doctoral program emphasizing original research in computer science and related fields.
academic_programs.curriculumSummary: Independent research under faculty supervision, limited coursework, and teaching opportunities.
academic_programs.entryRequirements: Master’s degree in computer science or closely related field; research proposal and supervisor agreement.

academic_programs.name: BSc in Data Science
academic_programs.overview: Undergraduate program integrating statistics, computing, and domain knowledge.
academic_programs.curriculumSummary: Courses in probability, statistics, programming, databases, and introductory machine learning; team-based projects.
academic_programs.entryRequirements: Secondary school diploma with mathematics and one science subject.

academic_programs.name: Graduate Certificate in AI Ethics and Policy
academic_programs.overview: Part-time certificate program exploring ethical, legal, and policy dimensions of AI.
academic_programs.curriculumSummary: Seminars on algorithmic fairness, data governance, and AI regulation; capstone policy brief.
academic_programs.entryRequirements: Completed bachelor’s degree and professional experience in technology, law, or public policy.

academic_programs.name: MSc in Statistics and Data Modelling
academic_programs.overview: Training in modern statistical methods for data analysis and modelling.
academic_programs.curriculumSummary: Generalized linear models, Bayesian statistics, time series analysis, computational statistics, and consulting practicum.
academic_programs.entryRequirements: Bachelor’s degree in mathematics, statistics, or related; strong background in probability and calculus.

academic_programs.name: MSc in Climate Change Science and Policy
academic_programs.overview: Interdisciplinary program focusing on climate science, adaptation, and policy instruments.
academic_programs.curriculumSummary: Climate modelling, environmental economics, international climate negotiations, and project-based learning with NGOs.
academic_programs.entryRequirements: Bachelor’s degree in environmental science, economics, or related; statement of purpose.

donation_funds.name: Student Scholarships
donation_funds.description: Provides need-based and merit scholarships to undergraduate and graduate students across all disciplines.

donation_funds.name: Research Innovation Fund
donation_funds.description: Supports high-risk, high-reward research projects and seed funding for new collaborations.

donation_funds.name: Neuroscience Discovery Fund
donation_funds.description: Advances cutting-edge neuroscience research, equipment, and trainee support.

donation_funds.name: Library Collections and Digital Access
donation_funds.description: Expands print and digital collections and improves access to research resources in the Main Research Library.

donation_funds.name: Institute General Fund
donation_funds.description: Flexible support used where the need is greatest across the institute.

faculty_members.biography: Ananya Bose leads the Fair ML group, focusing on algorithmic fairness, robustness, and trustworthy AI for healthcare applications.
Lars Engel works on robust AI methods for climate risk modelling and large-scale time series forecasting.
Clara Nguyen’s work focuses on reproducible data science workflows and open research software.
Marco Rossi develops machine learning models to study large-scale brain connectivity and neuroplasticity.
Julia Meier studies regional impacts of climate change and adaptation strategies for coastal communities.
Sophia Keller teaches applied statistics and works on methods for causal inference in observational data.
David Chen applies deep learning methods to genomic data to uncover disease-associated variants.

faculty_members.positionTitle: Associate Professor of Natural Language Processing
faculty_members.biography: Elena García’s research focuses on NLP for scientific and technical text, with an emphasis on domain adaptation.
Michael Owens oversees the Main Research Library and leads initiatives in digital collections and research data services.
Rachel Adams works on experimental quantum optics and supervises several interdisciplinary projects.
Tobias Hoffmann develops non-invasive brain-computer interfaces for communication and rehabilitation.
Yara Sanchez uses data science methods to study social inequality and public policy.
Peter Liu’s research focuses on reinforcement learning and sequential decision making under uncertainty.
Mina El-Sayed designs human-centered AI systems to support teaching and learning.

grants.title: Early-Career Data Science Collaboration Grant
grants.description: Supports early-career researchers building cross-disciplinary data science collaborations within the institute.

grants.title: Neuroscience Frontiers Early-Career Award
grants.description: Funding for innovative neuroscience projects led by early-career investigators.

grants.title: Sustainable AI Early-Career Challenge Fund
grants.description: Supports early-career researchers developing energy-efficient and sustainable AI methods.

grants.title: Interdisciplinary Research Seed Grants (Early-Career)
grants.description: Seed funding for interdisciplinary teams led by early-career PIs across faculties.

grants.title: Senior Research Chair in Computational Science
grants.description: A prestigious chair for established leaders in computational science.

grants.title: Open Small Projects Fund
grants.description: Micro-grants for pilot studies open to all institute researchers.

grants.title: Doctoral Fellowships in Data Science
grants.description: Stipends and tuition support for PhD students in data science.

grants.title: Postdoctoral Bridge Funding Scheme
grants.description: Short-term bridge funding for postdoctoral researchers between grants.

grants.title: Early-Career Microgrants for Pilot Studies
grants.description: Microgrants for early-career researchers to collect preliminary data.

grants.title: Flagship Early-Career Award in AI and Society
grants.description: Large-scale funding for transformative projects on the societal impact of AI.

news_articles.title: Institute Accelerates Climate Change Action with New Emissions Targets
news_articles.subtitle: Campus-wide commitment aims to halve emissions by 2030.
news_articles.body:
The institute has announced an ambitious climate action plan that sets new science-based emissions targets. The plan includes building retrofits, renewable energy procurement, and expanded support for climate change research.

Faculty and students will play a key role through new climate innovation grants and community partnerships.

news_articles.title: Public Forum Explores Local Impacts of Climate Change
news_articles.subtitle: Researchers and community leaders discuss adaptation strategies.
news_articles.body:
In a packed auditorium, institute scientists joined local officials and residents for a public forum on the local impacts of climate change. Speakers highlighted increased flood risks, heatwaves, and opportunities for community-led adaptation.

The event is part of an ongoing series designed to bring climate research into public conversation.

news_articles.title: Students Map Urban Heat Islands to Tackle Climate Change
news_articles.subtitle: Interdisciplinary course pairs data science with climate action.
news_articles.body: Students in the new Urban Climate Lab course are using satellite data and on-the-ground sensors to map heat islands around the city. Their findings will inform local planning decisions and public health interventions.

news_articles.title: New Center for Responsible AI Launched
news_articles.subtitle: Institute expands research on the societal impact of artificial intelligence.
news_articles.body: The Center for Responsible AI will bring together researchers from computer science, philosophy, law, and social sciences to study the impacts of AI on society. The center will host seminars, workshops, and a visiting fellows program.

news_articles.title: Neuroscience Lab Awarded Major Grant to Study Memory
news_articles.subtitle: Five-year project will map the neural basis of episodic memory.
news_articles.body: A multidisciplinary team from the Center for Neuroscience has received a major grant to investigate the neural circuitry underlying episodic memory. The project will combine high-resolution brain imaging with computational modeling.

news_articles.title: Main Research Library Reopens After Major Renovation
news_articles.subtitle: New spaces support collaborative and interdisciplinary research.
news_articles.body: The Main Research Library has reopened with expanded study areas, collaboration rooms, and a new digital scholarship lab. The renovation also improves accessibility and energy efficiency.

news_articles.title: Data Science Master’s Alumni Lead in Industry and Research
news_articles.subtitle: Graduates of the Applied Data Science MSc report strong career outcomes.
news_articles.body: A recent survey of alumni from the Applied Data Science MSc program shows high employment rates in technology, finance, and public sector roles. Many graduates also continue to PhD programs at leading universities.

news_articles.title: Spring Public Lecture Series Announced
news_articles.subtitle: Talks will highlight breakthroughs in neuroscience, AI, and climate research.
news_articles.body: The institute’s popular public lecture series returns this spring with talks by leading researchers. Lectures are free and open to the public, with registration required through the Events portal.

news_articles.title: New Early-Career Grants Support Interdisciplinary Research
news_articles.subtitle: Funding & Grants office opens several calls for early-career scholars.
news_articles.body: The Funding & Grants office has opened new early-career grant calls in data science, neuroscience, and sustainable AI. Applicants can review eligibility criteria and apply via the Funding & Grants section.

news_articles.title: Institute Welcomes New Machine Learning Faculty
news_articles.subtitle: Three new hires strengthen the institute’s AI research.
news_articles.body: The Department of Computer Science has appointed new faculty members in machine learning and AI, expanding course offerings and research supervision capacity for students.

news_articles.title: New Online Tools Help Plan Your Campus Visit
news_articles.subtitle: Visitors can now schedule library and lab visits in advance.
news_articles.body: The Visit Us section has been updated with new planning tools, allowing visitors to view detailed opening hours and create personalized visit plans for campus locations like the Main Research Library.

news_articles.title: Donors Break Record in Support of Student Scholarships
news_articles.subtitle: Gifts to student scholarships reach an all-time high.
news_articles.body: In the past year, donors contributed a record amount to student scholarship funds, enabling the institute to support more students than ever before.

news_articles.title: New Collaboration List Feature Launches for Researchers
news_articles.subtitle: Faculty can now easily track potential collaborators across the institute.
news_articles.body: The People section now includes a Collaboration List feature, allowing researchers to bookmark faculty profiles and keep track of potential collaborators.

news_articles.title: AI Helps Unlock Hidden Collections in the Research Library
news_articles.subtitle: New tools improve discovery of archival materials.
news_articles.body: The Main Research Library is piloting AI-based tools to enhance search and description of archival collections, making it easier for researchers to find relevant materials.

news_articles.title: New Online Course Demystifies Climate Change Science
news_articles.subtitle: Free MOOC shares latest research with global learners.
news_articles.body: Researchers have launched a massive open online course that explains the basics of climate change science and showcases current research at the institute.

publications.title: Trustworthy Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare Systems
publications.abstract: This paper proposes a framework for evaluating the trustworthiness of AI systems deployed in clinical workflows, focusing on robustness, fairness, and interpretability.

publications.title: Robust Artificial Intelligence for Climate Risk Modeling
publications.abstract: We introduce robust deep learning methods for probabilistic climate risk modeling under data and model uncertainty.

publications.title: Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence for Education
publications.abstract: This article outlines design principles for human-centered AI systems that support, rather than replace, teachers in the classroom.

publications.title: Machine Learning of Large-Scale Brain Connectivity Patterns
publications.abstract: We present a machine learning framework for modeling large-scale functional connectivity from fMRI data.

publications.title: Neuroplasticity Across the Lifespan: Evidence from Longitudinal Imaging
publications.abstract: Longitudinal imaging data reveal patterns of neuroplasticity associated with healthy aging and cognitive decline.

publications.title: Deep Learning for Rare Variant Discovery in Genomic Data
publications.abstract: We develop a deep generative model for detecting rare variants in whole-genome sequencing data.

publications.title: Practices and Pitfalls of Reproducible Data Science
publications.abstract: A survey of common practices and challenges in making data science workflows reproducible.

publications.title: Machine Learning Projections of Regional Climate Change Impacts
publications.abstract: We use machine learning models to project regional climate impacts under different emissions scenarios.

publications.title: Advanced Methods for Multi-Modal Neuroscience Data Integration
publications.abstract: We review and benchmark methods for integrating multi-modal neuroscience datasets.

publications.title: Building Sustainable Digital Archives in Academic Libraries
publications.abstract: This paper discusses strategies for sustainable digital preservation and access in research libraries.

publications.title: Graphical Models for Practical Causal Inference
publications.abstract: An accessible introduction to graphical models and causal inference for applied researchers.

publications.title: Measuring Fairness in Sequential Decision-Making Systems
publications.abstract: We propose new metrics and evaluation protocols for fairness in sequential decision-making scenarios.

publications.title: Domain Adaptation for Scientific Text Summarization
publications.abstract: We evaluate domain adaptation strategies for abstractive summarization of scientific articles.
publications.journalOrVenue: Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing

publications.title: Data Science for Social Good: Lessons from Community Partnerships
publications.abstract: We reflect on a series of data science projects conducted with community organizations and identify key success factors.

publications.title: Non-Invasive Brain-Computer Interfaces for Communication
publications.abstract: We review recent advances in non-invasive BCIs for communication in patients with severe motor impairments.

publications.title: Internal Report: Applying Artificial Intelligence to Library Operations
publications.abstract: An internal assessment of AI tools for cataloging, search, and user support within the Main Research Library.

research_labs.name: Cognitive Neuroscience Lab
research_labs.shortDescription: Investigating the neural basis of memory, attention, and decision making.
research_labs.fullDescription: The Cognitive Neuroscience Lab combines behavioral experiments, neuroimaging, and computational modeling to study how the brain supports cognition. Projects range from basic mechanisms of memory encoding to applied work on aging and neurodegenerative disease.

research_labs.name: Systems Neuroscience and BCI Lab
research_labs.shortDescription: Studying large-scale brain networks and brain-computer interfaces.
research_labs.fullDescription: The Systems Neuroscience and BCI Lab focuses on understanding large-scale neural dynamics and translating this knowledge into brain-computer interface technologies. The lab collaborates with clinical partners to develop communication tools for patients.

research_labs.name: Machine Learning and Reasoning Group
research_labs.shortDescription: Developing scalable and trustworthy machine learning algorithms.
research_labs.fullDescription: The Machine Learning and Reasoning Group works on core machine learning, probabilistic reasoning, and trustworthy AI. The group collaborates with application domains including healthcare, climate science, and digital libraries.

research_labs.name: Data Science Methods Lab
research_labs.shortDescription: Improving the reliability and reproducibility of data science.
research_labs.fullDescription: The Data Science Methods Lab develops tools and practices for robust, transparent, and reproducible data science. Projects include workflow tooling, benchmarking datasets, and education initiatives.

research_labs.name: Climate Change Impact Modeling Lab
research_labs.shortDescription: Modeling regional climate impacts and adaptation strategies.
research_labs.fullDescription: The Climate Change Impact Modeling Lab integrates climate models, machine learning, and socio-economic data to understand and mitigate regional climate risks.

research_labs.name: AI Ethics and Society Lab
research_labs.shortDescription: Exploring the ethical and societal implications of AI systems.
research_labs.fullDescription: The AI Ethics and Society Lab brings together researchers from computer science, philosophy, law, and social sciences to study fairness, accountability, transparency, and governance of AI.

research_labs.name: Quantum Physics and Optics Lab
research_labs.shortDescription: Fundamental research in quantum optics and photonics.
research_labs.fullDescription: The Quantum Physics and Optics Lab studies quantum states of light, quantum communication, and photonic devices, often in collaboration with engineering teams.

research_labs.name: Computational Genomics and Bioinformatics Lab
research_labs.shortDescription: Developing computational methods for genomic data analysis.
research_labs.fullDescription: The Computational Genomics and Bioinformatics Lab focuses on algorithms and software for analyzing large-scale genomic and transcriptomic datasets.

research_labs.name: Urban Sustainability Lab
research_labs.shortDescription: Research on sustainable cities, transport, and energy systems.
research_labs.fullDescription: The Urban Sustainability Lab collaborates with local governments and NGOs to develop data-driven solutions for sustainable urban development and climate resilience.

research_labs.name: Digital Libraries and Knowledge Infrastructures Lab
research_labs.shortDescription: Designing digital library systems and scholarly infrastructures.
research_labs.fullDescription: The Digital Libraries Lab works closely with the Main Research Library to build platforms for digital collections, data repositories, and open scholarship.

lab_projects.title: Neural Signatures of Episodic Memory Formation
lab_projects.summary: fMRI and behavioral experiments to characterize neural patterns associated with successful encoding and retrieval of episodic memories in healthy adults.

lab_projects.title: Attention Networks in Complex Visual Environments
lab_projects.summary: Examining how fronto-parietal networks support selective attention during realistic, cluttered visual scenes.

lab_projects.title: Memory Decline and Compensation in Healthy Aging
lab_projects.summary: Longitudinal study of memory performance and compensatory brain activity in older adults.

lab_projects.title: Non-Invasive Brain-Computer Interfaces for Communication
lab_projects.summary: Developing EEG-based BCIs to restore communication for patients with severe motor impairments.

lab_projects.title: Closed-Loop BCIs for Motor Rehabilitation
lab_projects.summary: Testing closed-loop stimulation protocols to enhance motor recovery after stroke.

lab_projects.title: Large-Scale Brain Networks During Sleep
lab_projects.summary: Analyzing multi-site EEG recordings to understand how large-scale networks reorganize across sleep stages.

lab_projects.title: Open Pipeline for High-Density Neural Data
lab_projects.summary: Building an open-source pipeline for preprocessing and sharing high-density neural recordings.

lab_projects.title: Robust Machine Learning for Climate Risk Models
lab_projects.summary: Designing robust deep learning architectures to improve reliability of climate risk projections under distributional shift.

lab_projects.title: Trustworthy AI for Clinical Decision Support
lab_projects.summary: Developing interpretable and fair ML models for clinical decision support systems in hospitals.

lab_projects.title: Reproducible Data Science Workflows
lab_projects.summary: Creating tooling and best practices for end-to-end reproducibility of complex data science projects.

lab_projects.title: Community Benchmark Datasets for Social Data Science
lab_projects.summary: Curating vetted, well-documented benchmark datasets for evaluating social data science methods.

lab_projects.title: Urban Heat Islands and Public Health
lab_projects.summary: Combining satellite observations and sensor networks to map urban heat islands and assess health risks.

lab_projects.title: Regional Flood Risk under Climate Change
lab_projects.summary: Developing high-resolution flood risk models under multiple climate and land-use scenarios.

lab_projects.title: Fairness and Transparency Toolkit for ML Practitioners
lab_projects.summary: Co-designing a toolkit with practitioners to assess fairness and transparency of deployed machine learning systems.

lab_projects.title: Policy Guidelines for Responsible AI Adoption
lab_projects.summary: Working with policymakers to translate technical AI ethics research into actionable policy guidelines.

lab_projects.title: Highly Efficient Single-Photon Sources for Quantum Communication
lab_projects.summary: Experimental development of bright, on-demand single-photon sources for secure quantum communication.

lab_projects.title: Rare Variant Discovery in Complex Disease Cohorts
lab_projects.summary: Applying deep learning and statistical genetics methods to whole-genome sequencing data from complex disease cohorts.

lab_projects.title: Sustainable Urban Mobility Scenarios
lab_projects.summary: Modeling the impact of different mobility policies on emissions, congestion, and equity in urban areas.

lab_projects.title: AI-Assisted Discovery in Archival Collections
lab_projects.summary: Deploying and evaluating machine learning tools to improve search and description of archival collections in the Main Research Library.

lab_projects.title: Next-Generation Research Data Repository
lab_projects.summary: Designing an institutional repository for research data with rich metadata, preservation workflows, and researcher-facing tools.

event_registrations.notes: Attending with a colleague from Philosophy.
Bringing two senior students interested in neuroscience.

events.title: Public Forum: Local Impacts of Climate Change
events.subtitle: Researchers and community leaders discuss adaptation strategies
events.description: A public forum bringing together institute scientists, local officials, and residents to discuss how climate change is affecting the region and what adaptation strategies are available.

events.title: Evening Public Lecture: Why AI Ethics Matters
events.subtitle: From algorithmic bias to accountability
events.description: In this public lecture, experts from the Center for Responsible AI explain why ethics must be at the heart of artificial intelligence development and deployment.

events.title: Hands-on Workshop: Reproducible Data Science Workflows
events.subtitle: From notebooks to pipelines
events.description: A full-day, hands-on workshop led by the Data Science Methods Lab on designing and maintaining reproducible data science workflows using version control, containers, and continuous integration.

events.title: Public Lecture: How the Brain Remembers
events.subtitle: What neuroscience reveals about human memory
events.description: Professor Marco Rossi explains recent discoveries about how the brain encodes and retrieves memories, and what this means for learning and aging. The lecture is designed for a general audience and will be followed by a Q&A.

events.title: Symposium on Climate Change and Artificial Intelligence
events.subtitle: From prediction to policy
events.description: This one-day symposium brings together climate scientists, machine learning researchers, and policymakers to explore how AI can support climate risk assessment and adaptation planning.

events.title: Public Lecture: The Science of Climate Change
events.subtitle: What we know and what we don’t
events.description: An introductory lecture on the science of climate change, covering evidence, projections, and common misconceptions.

events.title: Research Seminar: Robust ML for Sequential Decision Making
events.description: Peter Liu presents recent work on robust reinforcement learning under model misspecification.

events.title: Spring Conference on Data Science and Society
events.subtitle: Data for public good
events.description: A two-day conference showcasing data science projects with social impact, featuring keynote talks, panels, and poster sessions.

events.title: Workshop: Advanced Neuroimaging Methods
events.subtitle: Connectivity analysis and multi-modal integration
events.description: A technical workshop hosted by the Cognitive Neuroscience Lab on advanced methods for analyzing neuroimaging data, including connectivity and multi-modal integration.

events.title: Public Lecture: Climate Risks in Our Region
events.subtitle: From extreme heat to flooding
events.description: Climate scientist Julia Meier discusses projected climate risks in the region and how communities can prepare for extreme heat, flooding, and other impacts.

events.title: Public Lecture: Data Science for Social Good
events.subtitle: Lessons from community partnerships
events.description: Clara Nguyen and Yara Sanchez share experiences from data science projects conducted with community organizations, highlighting successes and challenges.

events.title: Public Lecture: AI and the Future of Research Libraries
events.subtitle: Unlocking hidden collections
events.description: Head Librarian Michael Owens explains how AI tools are being used to improve discovery of archival materials in the Main Research Library.

events.title: Neuroscience Colloquium: Systems Neuroscience and BCI
events.description: Tobias Hoffmann presents recent work on non-invasive brain-computer interfaces for communication.

events.title: Graduate Applications Workshop: Data Science and AI
events.subtitle: Tips from faculty and current students
events.description: An informational workshop for prospective applicants to Data Science and Machine Learning master’s programs, covering statement of purpose, CVs, and letters of recommendation.

events.title: Lunch Lecture: The Plastic Brain
events.subtitle: Neuroplasticity across the lifespan
events.description: A lunchtime lecture on neuroplasticity and how the brain changes in response to experience, aimed at a broad campus audience.

visiting_hours_rules.notes: Limited access to archives; check in advance for special collections.
Closed on Sundays except during exam periods.

campus_locations.name: Main Research Library
campus_locations.description: The central research library of the institute, offering quiet and collaborative study spaces, extensive print and digital collections, and dedicated support for researchers.
campus_locations.accessibilityNotes: Step-free access from the central quad, elevators to all floors, accessible restrooms, and assistive listening devices available at the information desk.

campus_locations.name: City Campus Auditorium
campus_locations.description: Large lecture hall on the City Campus used for public lectures, concerts, and major events.
campus_locations.accessibilityNotes: Ground-level entrances, wheelchair-accessible seating areas, and hearing loop installed.

campus_locations.name: Great Hall, Main Campus
campus_locations.description: Historic hall on the Main Campus hosting high-profile lectures and ceremonies.
campus_locations.accessibilityNotes: Ramp access via east entrance; accessible seating available upon request.

campus_locations.name: Data Science Center
campus_locations.description: Home of the Institute for Data Science, with teaching labs, seminar rooms, and collaboration spaces.
campus_locations.accessibilityNotes: Automatic doors at main entrance; elevators and accessible restrooms on each floor.

campus_locations.name: Neuroscience Building
campus_locations.description: Research and teaching building for the Center for Neuroscience, including imaging facilities and seminar rooms.
campus_locations.accessibilityNotes: Step-free entry and accessible routes to seminar rooms; imaging facilities have additional safety requirements.

campus_locations.name: Sustainability Campus Forum
campus_locations.description: Flexible event space on the Sustainability Campus used for symposia and public forums.
campus_locations.accessibilityNotes: Fully accessible venue with step-free access, accessible restrooms, and nearby reserved parking.

campus_locations.name: Main Conference Center
campus_locations.description: Dedicated conference facility on City Campus with multiple rooms for symposia, conferences, and workshops.
campus_locations.accessibilityNotes: All meeting rooms accessible; elevator access to upper levels; on-site signage indicates accessible routes.

campus_locations.name: Computer Science Building
campus_locations.description: Tech Park Campus building housing the Department of Computer Science and the Machine Learning and Reasoning Group.
campus_locations.accessibilityNotes: Elevator access from underground parking; accessible lecture theatres on levels 1 and 2.

campus_locations.name: City Hall Auditorium
campus_locations.description: Off-campus auditorium in the city center frequently used for joint institute–community events.
campus_locations.accessibilityNotes: Managed by the city; step-free side entrance and designated accessible seating.
